concerns.text: Task T036 (Sensitivity Analysis) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but explicitly depends on T022 (best model weights) and T026 (SHAP rankings). While T036 can run in parallel with T027/T029, the [P] tag implies it has no dependencies on other US3 tasks. More critically, T036 depends on T022 which is in US2. If US2 and US3 are run in parallel (as allowed by the plan), T036 cannot start until T022 completes. The [P] tag is misleading here as it suggests independence from the US2 completion gate.

Task T029 (Perturbation Study) depends on T022 (best model) and T026 (SHAP). However, T029 is listed as a separate task from T026, yet T026's description says it 'generates SHAP values... and create summary plots'. T029 requires the 'top SHAP-ranked descriptors' which are an output of T026. The dependency chain is correct (T026 -> T029), but T029 is marked as depending on T022 and T026, while T026 itself depends on T020/T021. This creates a long chain. The issue is that T029 is marked [P] in the list? No, T029 is not marked [P]. Wait, T029 is not marked [P]. The concern is about T036 being [P] when it depends on US2. Let's re-evaluate T036. T036 depends on T022 (US2) and T026 (US3). If US2 and US3 are parallel, T036 must wait for US2. The [P] tag is incorrect if it implies no cross-story dependency.

Task T035 (Consistency Check) depends on T020, T021, and T026. T026 (SHAP) depends on T020/T021. T035 re-runs training with 5 seeds. This implies T035 must wait for T020/T021 to complete to get the 'best configuration' to re-train. However, T035 is marked [P]? No, T035 is not marked [P]. The concern is about T036. Let's focus on T036. T036 is marked [P] but depends on T022 (US2). This is a cross-story dependency. The [P] tag is invalid for T036 because it cannot run in parallel with US2 tasks if US2 is not complete. The plan allows US2 and US3 to run in parallel, but T036 specifically needs the output of US2 (T022). Therefore, T036 cannot be [P] relative to the whole project, only relative to other US3 tasks. The tag is misleading.

Task T015 (Exclusion Report) depends on T012 and T013. T012 (Clean) and T013 (Descriptors) are both US1 tasks. T015 aggregates logs from T012 and T013. This is correct. However, T015 is not marked [P], which is correct. The issue is T016 (Save final dataset) depends on T015. This is correct. No ordering violation here.
Task T022 (Save best model) depends on T016 (cleaned dataset), T020 (training), and T021 (evaluation). T020 and T021 are US2 tasks. T016 is US1. This is correct. T022 is not marked [P]. No violation.

Task T036 is marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, it depends on T022 (best model from US2) and T026 (SHAP from US3). If the team executes US2 and US3 in parallel (as permitted by the plan), T036 cannot start until T022 completes. The [P] tag implies the task has no dependencies on other tasks in the same phase or story, or that it can run independently. Here, it has a hard dependency on a task in a different user story (US2). This violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule if interpreted as 'T036 can run in parallel with T022', which is false. The [P] tag is semantically incorrect for T036.

Task T036 depends on T022 (US2) and T026 (US3). T026 depends on T020/T021 (US2). So T036 depends on US2 completion. The [P] tag is invalid because T036 cannot run in parallel with the completion of US2. It must wait for T022. The tag suggests it can run in parallel with other tasks, but the dependency on T022 (which is in US2) means it cannot start until US2 is done. This is a hidden ordering requirement that the [P] tag obscures.
